market_notes: April 27, 2026: Markets at NEW ALL-TIME HIGHS for S&P 500 and Nasdaq (Motley Fool: 'S&P 500 Inches to New Record'). Barrons: 'Semiconductor Stocks End 18-Day Winning Streak. It's Time to Sell' — notable froth warning. TOMORROW April 29 MEGA-CAP EARNINGS: GOOG, META, MSFT, AMZN, KLAC all reporting. Portfolio at +10.3% P&L. Benchmarks: SPY +0.9% 1W, QQQ +2.7% 1W, SMH +9.1% 1W — continue to significantly outperform on semiconductor concentration. SNDK/WDC earnings April 30 — Morgan Stanley previewing strong SNDK quarter, BofA bullish on WDC. Druckenmiller reportedly sold SNDK — monitoring. DELL BofA positive on agentic AI CPU demand. Holding ~46% cash as strategic dry powder for post-mega-cap earnings deployment.
watching: MEGA-CAP EARNINGS April 29 (TOMORROW): GOOG, META, MSFT, AMZN, KLAC — AI capex commentary is #1 market driver; strong beats = aggressive redeployment into MRVL adds, ADI, AMAT, potential ARM add

Post-mega-cap earnings deployment targets: ADI at -3.9% from 52W high, AMAT at -3.7% from 52W high, MPWR at -4.5% from 52W high (reports April 30 — binary), potential MRVL/ARM adds on AI capex confirmation
Barrons 'Semiconductor Stocks End 18-Day Winning Streak. It's Time to Sell' — froth warning; monitoring if rotation out of semis accelerates post-earnings

recent_runs.reasoning: **Macro:** Market is in a strong risk-on regime with Nasdaq extending to a 10-day winning streak (IBD confirms), SMH +13.0% 1W and +16.7% 1M leading all benchmarks massively on AI infrastructure mega-rally. Iran war talks showing 'optimism' per Motley Fool headline today — markets erasing Iran war losses. Big banks (JPM beat, C beat, GS beat) confirming capital markets reactivation thesis. ASML earnings TODAY (April 15) and TSM earnings TOMORROW (April 16) are the critical binary events. **Sectors:** Semiconductors remain dominant — MRVL +22.4% 1W, MU +23.3% 1W, SNDK +32.9% 1W, AMD +15.1% 1W all crushing benchmarks. Memory storage supercycle intact with new Wall Street superbull on SNDK (Barrons: 'SanDisk Can Gain Another 175%'). Software sector bouncing — CRWD, SNOW, OKTA all rebounding per Yahoo Finance 'software stocks finally joining tech rally.' AVGO getting fresh Meta partnership catalyst (Reuters: Meta inks deal with Broadcom for custom AI chips). ORCL surging +14.2% 1W on Bloom Energy deal. BE surging +61.2% 1W on energy/AI data center demand. **EARNINGS ALERT — REQUIRED RESPONSES:** (1) ASML reports TODAY April 15: Holding 1 share at +15.9% unrealized. ASML at -1.9% from 52W high means very limited upside surprise potential. CEO flagged chip trade risks per Simply Wall St. UBS named ASML among European leaders. Barrons: 'ASML Earnings Are Coming — What to Watch for in AI and Memory Chip Boom.' Stock has already moved massively (+16.2% 1W). With only 1 share remaining, the binary risk is minimal in dollar terms (~$1,518). HOLD this 1 share — trimmed appropriately in prior runs, the remaining position is a minimal risk expression and the thesis on AI capex is intact. If earnings disappoint on China guidance (MATCH Act), the loss is capped at $1,518. (2) TSM reports TOMORROW April 16: Holding 13 shares at +7.7% unrealized ($379.89 current vs $352.69 avg). TSM +10.0% 1W into print. Q1 was pre-announced at +35% YoY revenue — this removes the top-line binary. The key question is Q2 AI capex guidance. Motley Fool calls TSM 'the No. 1 Semiconductor Stock to Buy Before Earnings Season.' ASML earnings commentary today on AI/memory demand will provide read-through. HOLD all 13 shares — the pre-announcement de-risked the top-line binary, fwd P/E 20.6x on 20% revenue growth with 45% margins is compelling, and NVIDIA/MRVL demand signals strongly support strong Q2 guidance. **Positions:** Portfolio is performing exceptionally — total P&L +5.8% vs SPY +5.1% 1M, QQQ +6.0% 1M, SMH +16.7% 1M. Portfolio is tracking benchmarks but lagging SMH significantly. Stars: MRVL +34.0% (new catalyst — Google TPU talks make it 'must-buy'), SNDK +22.1% (Barrons superbull, Nasdaq-100 inclusion, 175% upside thesis), MU +26.6% (Lynx raises PT, aggressive buying suggested), WDC +20.4% (analysts positive on AI memory demand), AMD +15.8%. ZS short -0.4% — barely losing, thesis intact on AI disruption, software sector bouncing is a risk but ZS is -63.6% from 52W high and Zacks compares PANW vs ZS unfavorably for ZS. AVGO +8.6% — just got a fresh Meta custom AI chip deal catalyst, this is a strong add opportunity. New opportunities: ORCL +14.2% 1W, 2.33x vol ratio — Oracle buying Bloom Energy fuel cell power for data centers (data center AI power demand), fwd P/E 20.4x on 22% revenue growth with 25% margins — compelling. ALAB +43.4% 1W, +41.8% 1M — Astera Labs explosive on AI custom silicon demand, -35.1% from 52W high means room to run. BE +61.2% 1W near 52W highs but overextended at 72x fwd P/E with negative margins. META +15.2% 1W, just partnered with AVGO AND CoreWeave — AI infrastructure spend confirming. AMZN +16.5% 1W — $11.6B Globalstar deal, fwd P/E 26.5x on 14% revenue growth, approaching 52W highs. Considering adding AVGO (fresh Meta catalyst, only 8 shares) and potentially ORCL (data center power play). ZS short — software sector bouncing broadly is a risk; Wedbush 'buy the dip' call gaining traction; however ZS is -13.7% 1W, -63.6% from 52W high, and the AI disruption thesis for cybersecurity remains intact. The ZS stop at $132.01 provides protection. **Cash:** Holding $48,307 (~33% of total equity) after margin. This is appropriate dry powder for post-TSM earnings deployment tomorrow and monitoring ASML today. Can deploy more aggressively into confirmed winners after binary events clear.

**Macro:** Markets are at new highs — S&P 500 closed above 7,000 for the first time, Nasdaq hit records with NVDA on an 11-day winning streak. Iran peace talks optimism continues to drive risk-on. ASML reported TODAY (April 15) and the stock DROPPED post-earnings per Yahoo Finance/BofA CEO headline — 'ASML stock falls post-earnings.' TSM reports TOMORROW April 16. Benchmarks: SPY +3.5%/+4.9%, QQQ +5.2%/+6.3%, SMH +7.1%/+15.0% — we need to outperform on risk-adjusted basis. **Sectors:** Semiconductors still leading (SMH +7.1% 1W) but showing some rotation — memory stocks (SNDK -7% today per 24/7 Wall St., MU 'taking a breather') are pulling back after enormous runs. MRVL hitting another record close (+17.7% 1W) — Barrons 'Marvell Eeks Out Another Record Close.' AVGO rising on Meta AI chip partnership confirmation (+13.1% 1W). Energy/defense mixed. BE +45.7% 1W — parabolic, near ATH. ZS short at -7.2% unrealized loss — ZS moved against us but still below stop at $132.01; current price $131.01 is very close to our stop. Software still struggling (NOW -3.4% 1W, still -55.5% from 52W high). HOOD +21.6% 1W on SEC day trading rule change. **EARNINGS ALERT — REQUIRED RESPONSES:** (1) ASML reports TODAY April 15 — HELD 1 share at +13.1% unrealized ($1,481.77 current). Yahoo Finance headline: 'BofA CEO calls economy resilient, ASML stock falls post-earnings.' This is the critical signal — the stock is DOWN post-earnings. Vol ratio 2.23x is high (institutional activity on the print). The stock has moved from our avg of $1,309.81 to $1,481.77 — we've already captured most of the upside. With earnings now out and the reaction negative, the thesis is at risk of mean reversion. DECISION: SELL the 1 remaining share of ASML — the earnings are out, the reaction is negative ('stock falls post-earnings'), and holding a 1-share position post-earnings with negative reaction is not worth the risk. Lock in the +13.1% gain. (2) TSM reports TOMORROW April 16 — holding 13 shares at +6.4% unrealized ($375.10 current, avg $352.69). NVIDIA 11-day winning streak, IBD 'Nvidia Chipmaker On Tap,' Nasdaq/S&P at records — the setup for TSM earnings is still excellent. Q1 was pre-announced at +35%, removing top-line binary. Key question is Q2 guidance and AI capex commentary. Multiple headlines confirm AI chip demand supercycle intact. DECISION: HOLD all 13 shares into TSM earnings. The pre-announcement de-risked top-line, fwd P/E 20.3x on 20% revenue growth with 45% margins is compelling, and market is at record highs the day before the print — favorable backdrop. **Positions:** Portfolio is at +4.5% total P&L. Stars working: MRVL +34.8% (record close, Nvidia $2B investment confirmed), MU +24.0%, WDC +20.0%, AMD +17.2%, SNDK +15.3% (but -7% today, Evercore $2,600 target = 191% upside per Barchart keeping the thesis alive), NVDA +12.4%, ASML +13.1% (selling today). ZS short at -7.2% ($131.01 vs $132.01 stop) — dangerously close to stop; ZS -5.0% 1W means it's actually moving in our direction this week (we're short), which is good, but the position is still underwater from entry. The -7.2% P&L means ZS rallied from our $122.23 entry to $131.01. Wait — ZS short: we are SHORT at $122.23, current $131.01. Price went UP so our short is LOSING money. Vol ratio 1.23x and -5% this week means ZS fell this week, which is good. The stop is $132.01 — we are at $131.01, just $1 below the stop. Given ZS is this close to the stop and the software sector is mixed, I should consider covering ZS to avoid a stop-out with minimal control. However the stock IS declining (-5% this week, -14.8% 1M). I'll hold for now but watch closely. NEM -4.3% 1W and -0.7% unrealized — gold pulling back slightly as Iran peace talks reduce safe-haven demand. Might consider trimming. ORCL +4.2% unrealized — the data center power theme is still intact. HOOD +21.6% 1W on SEC day trading rule change — not held, missed opportunity. **New opportunity — HOOD:** HOOD at $87.32, +21.6% 1W, +16% 1M, 2.37x vol ratio (highest in watchlist). SEC just removed day trading limits — massive catalyst for retail trading platform. Fwd P/E 32.4x on 26% revenue growth with 42% margins and 22% ROE — actually reasonable. Vol ratio 2.37x shows institutional buying. This is a fresh catalyst with significant momentum. Could add a small position. Also watching TSLA +14.2% 1W, MSFT +9.8% 1W. LWLG +44.1% 1W, +58.7% 1M — photonics/AI play near ATH. NBIS +33.4% 1W near all-time highs. ALAB +37.2% 1W but was in watchlist; -34.5% from 52W high. **Cash:** Currently $44,771 cash (~30% of total equity after margin). After selling ASML 1 share (~$1,482 proceeds), cash increases to ~$46,253. This is appropriate — TSM binary event tomorrow, markets at record highs (crowded positioning risk), ZS short near stop, and NEM underperforming. Holding ~30% cash with optionality for post-TSM earnings deployment if strong guidance emerges. Not deploying aggressively into HOOD yet — the 21.6% 1W move already happened, and entering after a parabolic move on a single regulatory catalyst is chasing. Will reassess after TSM prints.

**Macro:** Markets at new record highs — S&P 500 and Nasdaq both hitting fresh ATHs with AMD closing at all-time high and TSM beating Q1 estimates decisively ('profit surged 58%', 'Beats First-Quarter Targets, Guides Up' per IBD). TSMC CEO dropped hints about AI stock moves — extremely bullish context. Iran peace talks ongoing, risk-on regime fully intact. Benchmarks: SPY +3.2%/+4.9%, QQQ +5.0%/+6.3%, SMH +5.7%/+14.6% — our portfolio needs to outperform on risk-adjusted basis.

**EARNINGS ALERT — REQUIRED RESPONSES:**
(1) TSM reports TODAY April 16: TSM beat Q1 estimates with profit up 58%, guided UP — this is a massive positive catalyst. IBD: 'Chip Foundry TSMC Beats First-Quarter Targets, Guides Up.' Motley Fool: 'This AI Chipmaker Profit Just Surged 58% — 3 Reasons to Buy.' TSM CEO 'dropped a hint about next move in AI stocks' (Yahoo Finance) — extremely bullish. Current: 13 shares at avg $352.69, current $363.35, +3.0% unrealized. The earnings beat + guidance raise + upward guidance is a clear ADD signal. Per my prior watching note: 'strong guidance = consider adding or holding.' I will ADD 5 shares to TSM — the binary risk has resolved positively, the thesis is confirmed, and the stock is positioned to rally on the open tomorrow.

(2) NEM reports April 23 — NEXT WEEK. Current: 15 shares at $113.79 avg, current $113.41, -0.3% unrealized. NEM is -4.7% 1W — gold is pulling back as Iran peace talks progress. Zacks: 'Wall Street Expects Earnings Growth.' Simply Wall St: '104% One Year Surge.' The gold thesis is dual: (a) peace talks = less safe-haven demand, and (b) rate cuts still possible. NEM has a stop at $104.69. With earnings April 23 and gold weakening (-4.7% 1W), I should consider trimming ahead of the binary. However NEM is only -0.3% unrealized and fwd P/E 10.3x on 21% revenue growth with 31% margins is extremely cheap. The stop provides protection. DECISION: HOLD all 15 shares into NEM earnings. The valuation is compelling, stop is in place, and gold miners tend to beat in rising gold price environments. -4.7% 1W is a concern but the long-term gold/rate thesis remains intact.

**Sectors:** Semiconductors continue to lead with AMD +17.6% 1W hitting all-time highs, TSM earnings beat, NVDA AI rally continuing. SNDK +8% 1W continuing memory supercycle. ZS stopped out at $134.33 — our short was closed by stop loss. Software sector now showing mixed signals with NOW +7.4% 1W (ServiceNow bouncing ahead of April 22 earnings). HOOD +23.9% 1W — still momentum but we missed the entry.

**Positions:** Portfolio at +4.6% total P&L with no open short positions (ZS stopped out). Stars working: MRVL +33.5% (Stifel raises target to $140, Nvidia joins Microsoft/Amazon in expanding MRVL's AI role), AMD +26.4% (closed at ATH, longest winning streak since 2005), SNDK +18.9% (memory supercycle intact), WDC +18.9%, MU +24.3%, ORCL +9.4% (AWS deal expansion fresh catalyst), AVGO +10.0% (8-day winning streak), ANET +11.0% (Rosenblatt AI confidence, Zacks Bull of the Day), NVDA +12.1% (AI powerhouse narrative intact). TSM +3.0% — about to re-rate significantly higher on the earnings beat+guide up. ARM +12.6% — Jim Cramer 'not impressed with MS coverage' but stock still performing. ZS stop-out note: We lost -9.9% on ZS. The short thesis was wrong — ZS is up 9.9% 1W and now +9.9% on the week. This was a painful stop-out. The position was stopped at $134.33. We have no short exposure currently.

The AWS multicloud deal is incremental to the Bloom Energy AI power deal we entered on. ORCL at $178 is -48% from 52W high — enormous recovery potential. Fwd P/E 22.4x on 22% revenue growth with 25% margins and 1.6x vol ratio. This is a strong hold.

**TSM Add Decision:** TSM at $363.35, earnings beat+guide up confirmed. Adding 5 shares at market for ~$1,817. This brings TSM to 18 shares total at avg ~$355.30. Total TSM position ~$6,500 = ~5.7% portfolio weight. Conviction: HIGH — the binary resolved positively with a 58% profit surge and upward guidance. The CEO commentary on AI stocks is bullish. This is the foundry backbone for all AI chip manufacturing.

**Cash:** Currently $52,133 (~48% of total equity approx). After adding TSM (~$1,817), cash goes to ~$50,316. Still ~46% cash. This is appropriate — we have a dense earnings calendar ahead (NOW April 22, TSLA April 22, VRT April 22, GEV April 22, then GOOG/META/MSFT/AMZN April 29) and need dry powder for post-earnings deployment. The TSM add is the one high-conviction action today given the confirmed earnings beat. No new shorts needed currently — software sector bouncing and no clear short setups with sufficient conviction.

**Macro:** Markets surging to new records — S&P 500 closed at all-time high, Dow +800 pts, Nasdaq +6.8% for the week driven by Iran Strait of Hormuz reopening (risk-on surge), TSM Q1 +58% profit beat with upward guidance, and AMD at all-time high. SPY +4.5% 1W, QQQ +6.2% 1W, SMH +6.2% 1W — our portfolio needs to outperform on risk-adjusted basis. The Iran peace/Hormuz reopening is a decisive macro positive removing the last major geopolitical overhang. Dense earnings week ahead: TSLA, VRT, GEV, LRCX, BSX, NOW on April 22; UNH, GE, NOC, EQT, HAL, RTX, ISRG on April 21; then GOOG/META/MSFT/AMZN/NVDA/KLAC April 29.

**Sectors:** Semiconductors continue leading (AMD +13.6% 1W at ATH, AVGO +9.4% 1W, MRVL +8.7% 1W at ATH per Barrons 'not done yet', NVDA +6.9% 1W) — Barrons confirms 'AI chips used to be Nvidia's game. Now more winners are emerging.' Software had its best week since 2001 per Barrons — NOW +16.5% 1W (we covered at +0.8%, good timing), SNOW +18.9% 1W, ZS +14.1% 1W. Software rally is real with narrative shifting on AI agents being complementary not disruptive. Energy stocks losing wartime gains as Hormuz reopens — energy is now a headwind. SNDK +8.1% 1W continuing, INTC soaring to 26-year high (+9.8% 1W, +52.1% 1M). NFLX -5.5% 1W on Netflix stock slide, Reed Hastings departure from board — potential short.

**EARNINGS ALERT — NEM reports April 23:** NEM is at $116.50, +2.4% unrealized. The news is concerning: '24/7 Wall St: Newmont Downgraded as Costs Surge: Is This Gold Giant Losing Its Shine at the Worst Possible Time?' and 'Simply Wall St: Does Persian Gulf Shock Expose a Deeper Volatility Challenge in Newmont's Gold Model?' The Hormuz reopening/Iran peace removes the safe-haven bid for gold — NEM -3.6% 1W reflects this. With earnings April 23, a cost-surge downgrade narrative, and gold losing its geopolitical premium, I should TRIM NEM from 15 to 8 shares — lock in partial profit on the +2.4% gain, reduce binary earnings risk with a negative pre-earnings narrative forming around cost overruns. The stop at $104.69 provides downside protection but the downgrade is a specific concern. DECISION: TRIM to 8 shares, keeping partial exposure but reducing risk ahead of April 23 earnings with negative momentum.

**Positions:** Portfolio at +5.7% total P&L with 13 positions. Stars: MRVL +39.9% (ATH, Barrons 'not done yet', Stifel $140 target), AMD +26.4% (ATH, longest winning streak since 2005), MU +23.7% (massive earnings beat confirmed), WDC +22.5%, SNDK +19.1%, ANET +13.2%, AVGO +12.2% (93% margin software machine powering AI bets), NVDA +14.0%, ARM +15.7%. TSM +4.2% — flat this week (-0.0% 1W) despite massive earnings beat; some profit-taking but thesis intact with 18 shares. ORCL +7.4% — Oracle surging +26.8% 1W on beaten-down tech re-rating, Investopedia confirms the rally. GS +7.6% — beat confirmed, capital markets thesis played out perfectly. The Hormuz reopening has eliminated energy sector's wartime premium — I have no energy exposure so this is neutral. Software sector's best week since 2001 validates our decision to cover NOW at +0.8% — we would have been badly squeezed.

**New opportunities:** INTC at $68.50, +9.8% 1W, +52.1% 1M, soaring to 26-year high. Reports April 23 — earnings risk. Pass for now due to binary risk. NFLX -5.5% 1W, 2.91x vol ratio (highest significant name in watchlist), Reed Hastings departure from board, forecast misses — potential SHORT. NFLX at fwd P/E 25.1x on 18% revenue growth is not cheap, and the CEO founder departure is a major negative catalyst. However stock is down 5.5% already this week — may be priced in. HOOD +31.2% 1W — extremely parabolic, we missed the entry. APLD +20.1% 1W — AI cloud infrastructure interesting but -25% from 52W high and speculative. APO +19.5% 1W on financial sector momentum — interesting but very extended. APP +21.9% 1W — AppLovin on fire with 61% margins and 66% revenue growth, fwd P/E 23.6x is actually reasonable for the growth rate. This is a compelling momentum add — but reports earnings April 28, creating binary risk. TSLA +14.8% 1W reports Tuesday April 22 — not held, watching. VRT +4.1% 1W reports Tuesday April 22 — not held, watching for AI capex read-through. MSFT +14.0% 1W — massive move, held by long-term agent. PLTR +14.3% 1W — Palantir surging, fwd P/E 78.6x too expensive. DDOG +20.2% 1W — Datadog recovery interesting at fwd P/E 48x with 29% revenue growth.

**Actions today:** (1) TRIM NEM from 15 to 8 shares — reduce binary earnings risk with cost-surge downgrade narrative. (2) Consider adding ORCL — +26.8% 1W, our 10 shares at +7.4% unrealized, the Investopedia/Barrons narrative on beaten-down tech re-rating is strong. However ORCL already moved significantly this week; hold current position. (3) No new shorts — software sector bouncing strongly (best week since 2001), narrative is shifting to AI-complementary not disruptive. Energy short tempting but no clear setup in universe. (4) TSM at -0.0% 1W despite 58% profit surge — the post-earnings price action is flat/muted. This may be profit-taking from the pre-earnings run-up. Thesis intact but no add needed with 18 shares already.

**Cash:** Currently $50,317 (~46% of portfolio). After trimming 7 NEM shares (~$815.50 proceeds), cash goes to ~$51,133 (~47%). Holding elevated cash is justified: dense earnings week starting April 21 with major names reporting (UNH, GE, NOC, RTX Monday; TSLA, VRT, GEV, LRCX Tuesday; then mega-cap week April 29). This is strategic dry powder for post-earnings deployment in confirmed winners. The risk of deploying into new names ahead of a massive earnings cluster is high — I want capacity to add to existing winners on earnings beats or initiate new positions on post-earnings confirmation.

**Macro:** Markets are at record highs but showing a pause today — Nasdaq snapped its longest win streak since 1992, S&P/Nasdaq pulling back from ATHs amid renewed Iran/Hormuz tensions (IBD: 'Nasdaq Snaps Win Streak On Rising U.S.-Iran Tensions'). Oil jumping again on Strait of Hormuz uncertainty creates cross-currents: energy/defense getting a bid but tech/semis facing headwinds. SPY +3.3% 1W, QQQ +4.8% 1W, SMH +4.7% 1W — our portfolio needs to outperform on risk-adjusted basis. The risk-on regime is intact but Iran re-escalation is a genuine tail risk. A major AAPL headline just dropped: Tim Cook stepping down, to be succeeded by John Ternus — this is a significant near-term catalyst for AAPL.

**Sectors:** Semiconductors still leading but Yahoo Finance warns 'Semiconductor stocks haven't been this hot since the dot-com bubble — and it could end badly' — a notable froth warning. MRVL is exceptional: Barrons 'How Google Is Playing Semiconductor Kingmaker—and Making Marvell a Winner' plus Amazon-Anthropic $25B partnership read-through. AMD +11.4% 1W, ARM +11.1% 1W both continuing strongly with IBD 'AI-Driven CPU Renaissance to Boost AMD, Arm, Intel.' ANET: Evercore adds to tactical outperform ahead of earnings — bullish. Software sector is rebounding: NOW +12% 1W (we covered at +0.8% — good), SNOW +12.1% 1W, DDOG +17.9% 1W. Defense/energy: RTX reports April 21, LMT reports April 23 — defense getting a bid on Iran re-escalation. AAPL Tim Cook departure is a major event — leadership transition uncertainty.

**Positions — EARNINGS ALERT: NEM reports April 23:** NEM is at $114.84, +0.9% unrealized. News is very negative: 'U.S. mining stocks dive as gold and other metals under pressure on Iran tensions' (Investing.com), 'Newmont Operational Setbacks Test Cost Discipline And Margin Resilience' (Simply Wall St.), and 'Barrick Mining vs. Newmont: Which Gold Giant Is Shining Brighter?' — the comparison article suggests Barrick may be outperforming. NEM is -1.4% 1W and gold is under pressure as Iran tensions create a complex dynamic (oil spike but also risk-off gold bid being offset by dollar strength). The cost discipline concerns from Simply Wall St. combined with the downgrade thesis from prior context is concerning heading into April 23 earnings. I already trimmed from 15 to 8 shares. DECISION: EXIT remaining 8 NEM shares. The negative pre-earnings narrative (operational setbacks, cost discipline issues, gold under pressure from Iran tensions creating oil-positive/gold-complex environment) combined with the -1.4% 1W trend and the prior downgrade suggests the risk/reward is poor. Lock in the +0.9% unrealized gain before binary earnings risk materializes.

**Other positions:** MRVL +48.0% is exceptional — Barrons and Google AI partnership news confirm the thesis is intact and potentially accelerating. No reason to trim given positive catalyst flow. AMD +24.9%, strong momentum continuing with CPU Renaissance narrative. NVDA +14.2%, thesis intact. MU +21.9% — Wells Fargo note on Micron/Sandisk looking risky is worth watching but Motley Fool $600 target thesis remains. ARM +21.5% — new telco AI server collaboration (+5.8% catalyst per Simply Wall St.). ANET +15.0% — Evercore tactical outperform add is a fresh catalyst ahead of earnings. WDC +23.0%, SNDK +18.1% — memory thesis intact though 'Micron and Sandisk stocks look risky right now' (Yahoo Finance) and SNDK -4.1% 1W warrants watching. GS +9.5% — post-earnings holding well at +9.5% unrealized. TSM +3.0% — flat post-earnings beat, steady.

**New opportunities:** (1) AAPL: Tim Cook stepping down to be succeeded by John Ternus — this is a major leadership transition. Short-term this creates uncertainty (fwd P/E 29.1x on 16% revenue growth). However, the stock is only -5.3% from 52W high and Goldman says 'overly pessimistic' concerns. Not a clear short or long with unclear direction. Pass. (2) DDOG: +17.9% 1W, -35.7% from 52W high, fwd P/E 49.2x on 29% revenue growth with software sector rebounding strongly. Zacks notes it's a potential earnings beat candidate. No earnings imminent in next 14 days per list. The fwd P/E 9.0x on 61% growth means even a slight beat is asymmetrically positive. WDC reports April 30: Holding 2 shares at avg $304.15, current $400.73, +31.8% unrealized. BofA today: 'Seagate, Western Digital Stocks Have More Room to Run.' Investopedia: 'Data Storage Stocks Have Had a Great Year So Far. It Could Be About to Get Better.' Jim Cramer: 'Can Keep Blowing Away the Numbers.' However: Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller reportedly SOLD Sandisk position (Motley Fool: 'Billionaire Stanley Druckenmiller Sells Sandisk and Buys an Unstoppable AI Energy Stock'). This is a notable negative signal. WDC at $400.73, Barclays PT $405 — still essentially at the PT. With only 2 shares and the PT essentially hit, the risk/reward is balanced but I'll HOLD given the BofA and Cramer bullish commentary on storage. The Druckenmiller SNDK sale is more concerning for SNDK but I note it's a trimming signal rather than bearish thesis change. **ANET critical update:** IBD reports ANET lost Meta AND Oracle deals to NVIDIA. This is a significant competitive loss. ANET is at $172.47, only +3.4% 1W — already underperforming. Our position is at +18.9% unrealized with 20 shares at $145.07. The losing of two major hyperscaler deals to NVDA is a material negative thesis change. The stock is already at -4.1% from 52W high. With this negative catalyst, I should TRIM ANET — selling 10 shares (half the position) to lock in gains while maintaining some exposure in case the news is overdone. The 'rebounds after' in the IBD headline suggests some recovery but the deal loss is real. MRVL update: 'POET canceled orders' is actually MRVL canceling its OWN orders, suggesting MRVL is moving its optical interconnect in-house or changing suppliers. Our MRVL position is at +58.4% unrealized — extraordinary gain. The POET deal breakdown could indicate MRVL is restructuring its supply chain, which is ambiguous for MRVL itself. However, with mega-cap earnings tomorrow and the Celestial AI deal breakdown creating uncertainty, I'll hold MRVL as the core AI networking thesis remains intact (Amazon, Google still dependent on MRVL chips). DELL update: BofA today says 'Dell, Hewlett Packard Enterprise Positioned to Benefit From Agentic AI-Driven CPU Demand' — positive read-through. DELL at only +0.6% unrealized is still underperforming but the agentic AI CPU narrative is a fresh positive catalyst. HOLD. NVDA: At -0.1% from 52W high, essentially at all-time high. SpaceX buying $60B AI (Motley Fool). Approaching ATH is a moment to potentially add more given the extraordinary momentum. **New opportunities:** GOOG at -0.7% from 52W high, reporting tomorrow — binary risk too high.
